guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: The front half has at least two remembered-by-feel proof frames and one route/readability payoff without crowding the opening.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Add only the state/test hooks needed to prove front-half return, route, and support behavior after tactile edits.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Refresh Beach or Coastal Scrub relationship copy in one compact note-backed place cluster, avoiding duplicate field-guide facts.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Polish one front-half physical beat or transition silhouette, using existing traversal language and no new system.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.focus: Make the live Coastal Scrub opening carry one tiny, optional back-dune shelter beat so the front-half route has a remembered-by-feel physical anchor.
current_state: lane 2 tightened the `shelter-builds-here` back-dune note around beach grass, sand verbena, and dune lupine
lane 4 proved the existing `Open To Shelter` support/readiness behavior
the beach-to-scrub corridor already has a held back-dune shelf, but the live Coastal Scrub opening has no authored back-dune starter shelf before the larger windbreak/swale platform family
main_acceptance: add one tiny optional authored back-dune starter shelf/hold in `coastal-scrub.ts` using existing `drift-platform` language
keep the beat inside the `back-dune` zone before `shrub-thicket`, shallow enough to read as a low hop rather than a new route gate
if stable carriers are added, use at most existing back-dune entries such as `beach-grass` and `sand-verbena` near the shelf
`coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts` pins the shelf ids, x/y relationship, zone bounds, and carrier ids/positions if added
no route definitions, support behavior, station pages, save schema, science copy, world-map behavior, corridor geometry, player physics, new UI, or broad traversal rewrite
baseline_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter|front-half-open-to-shelter|held back-dune shelf"
main_verification: focused runtime-smoke slice if runtime expectations are touched
browser proof under ignored `output/lane-3-main-360-browser/` if live geometry changes land

main_result.summary: Added a tiny optional authored back-dune shelter shelf/hold in the live Coastal Scrub opening, with stable beach-grass and sand-verbena carriers and focused biome-generation coverage.
guardrails_preserved: no route definitions, support behavior, station pages, save schema, science copy, world-map behavior, corridor geometry, player physics, new UI, or broad traversal rewrite changed
the new shelf stays optional, inside the `back-dune` zone, and before the later `windbreak-*` platform family
browser proof artifacts were captured under ignored `output/lane-3-main-360-browser/`
verified: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter|front-half-open-to-shelter|held back-dune shelf"

critic_result.summary: Reviewed the Coastal Scrub opening shelf cleanly. The pass stays lane-3 scoped, keeps the shelf optional and inside back-dune, preserves route/support/station/save/science/world-map/corridor/physics/UI behavior, and has focused test plus browser proof coverage.
verified: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter|front-half-open-to-shelter|held back-dune shelf"
reviewed npm run build result from main step
reviewed direct browser proof under output/lane-3-main-360-browser/

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Route-proof the front-half beat so support/readiness changes what the player notices during an existing route.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.focus: Make the first front-half `Open To Shelter` route handoff support-readable with focused proof, using existing hand-lens retargeting and note-tabs chip seams instead of new route/UI behavior.
current_state: lane 1 added the `front-half-open-to-shelter` debug snapshot for the first post-forest Coastal Scrub handoff
lane 2 refreshed the `shelter-builds-here` note around the back-dune `beach-grass`, `sand-verbena`, and `dune-lupine` tactile cluster
`coastal-shelter-shift` already starts `Open To Shelter` with the `open-bloom` slot on `sand-verbena`
hand lens can retarget to ordinary notebook-fit route clues and note-tabs can make the existing in-field chip progress-facing
main_acceptance: controller-level proof shows hand lens can retarget an active `Open To Shelter` back-dune inspect from a nearer non-fit candidate to the `sand-verbena` `open-bloom` clue
the same proof keeps that retarget ordinary, not process-backed: `supportRetargetsInspect` true, `supportPrefersActiveClue` false, `Notebook fit: open bloom`, and no `LENS CLUE` label
paired non-hand-lens coverage shows `note-tabs` leaves the physical nearest inspectable alone while keeping the in-field chip progress-facing
at most one focused runtime-smoke expectation proves the front-half support/readiness cue in play if a stable shelf or existing route path is practical

result: Added controller proof that `hand-lens` retargets active `Open To Shelter` back-dune inspection from a nearer non-fit `beach-pea` candidate to the farther `sand-verbena` `open-bloom` clue.
Confirmed the `hand-lens` retarget is ordinary notebook-fit behavior: `supportRetargetsInspect` true, `supportPrefersActiveClue` false, `Notebook fit: open bloom`, and no `LENS CLUE` label.
Added paired `note-tabs` coverage proving non-hand-lens support leaves the physical nearest inspectable alone while the in-field chip stays progress-facing with `0/3 stages`.

lane_2_critic_result.known_external_test_noise: Broad runtime-smoke and the route-board place-tab smoke case still fail on pre-existing world-map focus expectations before reaching this copy assertion.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
